subject: Sports
title: AC Milan vs Inter Milan: The Derby della Madonnina
description: A quick, practical guide to AC Milan vs Inter Milan: The Derby della Madonnina.

hook.text: Same city, same stadium, total war. Milan and Inter share San Siro but share nothing else.

buildup.text: Inter was founded in 1908 by breakaway members of AC Milan who wanted the club open to foreign players.

discovery.text: Inter became the 'international' club of immigrants. Milan kept its elite, establishment identity.

twist.text: They literally share a stadium—playing home games in alternating weeks at the 75,000-seat San Siro.

climax.text: Combined, they have won 10 European Cups. The derby decides which half of Milan owns bragging rights.

punchline.text: Same stadium, different worlds. Milan's derby is a civil war at home.

quiz.question: Why do AC Milan and Inter share a stadium?
options: Both clubs use the San Siro, alternating home games
One club rents from the other
The city only permits one football stadium
